we are reimbursed for travel expenses, lodging (S+z per

pay for myself. 
To receive reimbursement, I must attend
all meetings and sign attendance sheets several times a
day. Also, f must present an i.temized bill 
and receipts
for the above items. This totals approximately S900 per

Does the FPPC feel thls is a conflict of interest? would it

clude this when I file my candidate's finaL filing

the teachers of cafifornia at the National Education Asso-
ciation Resolutions Assembly in Kansas city, Missouri, in

City council election. A11 of the state Representatives to
the N.E.A./R.A., approximately 700 teachers, are receiving
funding of 5875 to cover the cost of travel, Iodging and
food. we assume any additional costs. I must attend aII
meetings and sign daily attendance sheets in order to

tainment is not included in this funding nor the State

sections.question:
1. I have been a teacher in California and a member of the California Teachers Association (C.T.A.) for 29 years. In April I was elected to the City Council of the City of Temple City and am fulfilling these obligations in addition to my full-time teaching job and my elected position on the C.T.A. governing body, State Council. As one of 500-plus elected members, I must attend eight (8) meetings a year to act on C.T.A. policy matters. Two of these meetings are in Northern California. Four of the meetings necessitate my spending the weekend at the hotel where the meetings are held. This is an elected position, but not a salaried position. I am currently serving on the second year of my second term. We are reimbursed for travel expenses, lodging ($42 per night) and food ($36 per day). Any other expenses I must pay for myself. To receive reimbursement, I must attend all meetings and sign attendance sheets several times a day. Also, I must present an itemized bill and receipts for the above items. This totals approximately $900 per year depending on air fare. Does the FPPC feel this is a conflict of interest? Would it be a reportable income on my Form 721? Do I need to include this when I file my candidate's final filing on July 31st? This income was never comingled with campaign funds and was not a part my campaign expenditures for my election to the City Council. If for some reason it should be included, how do I include this since it is not a donation, gift or honorarium?

2. I have a similar problem with my being elected to represent the teachers of California at the National Education Association Resolutions Assembly in Kansas City, Missouri, in July. I was elected to this position before the April 10th City Council election. All of the state Representatives to the N.E.A./R.A., approximately 700 teachers, are receiving funding of $875 to cover the cost of travel, lodging and food. We assume any additional costs. I must attend all meetings and sign daily attendance sheets in order to receive the funding. Any monies not used must be returned to C.T.A. (Last year my costs came to $1,000.00.) Entertainment is not included in this funding nor the State Council funding as these are business meetings. In addition, specific hotels and fees have been set up in advance. My job will be to vote on N.E.A. proposed amendments - Constitution, Bylaws and Standing Rules - and to elect new N.E.A. Officers. I feel that the N.E.A. has no bearing on Temple City or visa versa and no conflict of interest exists. But I ask for your interpretation so that I can correctly file my candidate's papers on July 31st and my Form 721 for 1990. Does this $875.00 of funding need to be included in my report and, if so, where do I include it? It is not a gift, donation or honorarium.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester, a newly elected Temple City Councilmember, seeks clarification on whether reimbursements for attending California Teachers Association ($900/year) and National Education Association meetings ($875) constitute reportable income, conflict of interest, and whether they must be disclosed on Form 721 and candidate filings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. The document contains two detailed questions (Q1 and Q2) but no conclusion or advice section. Document ID 90452 from 1990. Dated June 29, 1990, with July 31st deadline for candidate filings mentioned.

QUESTION: 1. I have been a teacher in California and a member of the California Teachers Association (C.T.A.) for 29 years. In April I was elected to the City Council of the City of Temple City and am fulfilling these obligations in addition to my full-time teaching job and my elected position on the C.T.A. governing body, State Council. As one of 500-plus elected members, I must attend eight (8) meetings a year to act on C.T.A. policy matters. Two of these meetings are in Northern California. Four of the meetings necessitate my spending the weekend at the hotel where the meetings are held. This is an elected position, but not a salaried position. I am currently serving on the second year of my second term. We are reimbursed for travel expenses, lodging ($42 per night) and food ($36 per day). Any other expenses I must pay for myself. To receive reimbursement, I must attend all meetings and sign attendance sheets several times a day. Also, I must present an itemized bill and receipts for the above items. This totals approximately $900 per year depending on air fare. Does the FPPC feel this is a conflict of interest? Would it be a reportable income on my Form 721? Do I need to include this when I file my candidate's final filing on July 31st? This income was never comingled with campaign funds and was not a part my campaign expenditures for my election to the City Council. If for some reason it should be included, how do I include this since it is not a donation, gift or honorarium?

2. I have a similar problem with my being elected to represent the teachers of California at the National Education Association Resolutions Assembly in Kansas City, Missouri, in July. I was elected to this position before the April 10th City Council election. All of the state Representatives to the N.E.A./R.A., approximately 700 teachers, are receiving funding of $875 to cover the cost of travel, lodging and food. We assume any additional costs. I must attend all meetings and sign daily attendance sheets in order to receive the funding. Any monies not used must be returned to C.T.A. (Last year my costs came to $1,000.00.) Entertainment is not included in this funding nor the State Council funding as these are business meetings. In addition, specific hotels and fees have been set up in advance. My job will be to vote on N.E.A. proposed amendments - Constitution, Bylaws and Standing Rules - and to elect new N.E.A. Officers. I feel that the N.E.A. has no bearing on Temple City or visa versa and no conflict of interest exists. But I ask for your interpretation so that I can correctly file my candidate's papers on July 31st and my Form 721 for 1990. Does this $875.00 of funding need to be included in my report and, if so, where do I include it? It is not a gift, donation or honorarium.

embedding.summary: A newly elected Temple City Councilmember who is also an elected member of the California Teachers Association and selected delegate to the National Education Association requests FPPC guidance on whether meeting-related reimbursements should be reported as income on Form 721 and candidate filings, and whether they constitute conflicts of interest.
